summary: EC2 instances. Resize is guardrailed (stop/start downtime); a tag add is self-service; a brand-new instance routes to an engineer.

operations.title: Resize an EC2 instance
operations.summary: Resize this instance to an approved size; applying stops and restarts it, so expect brief downtime.
operations.description: Change instance_type to an allowlisted size. Requires a stop/start — brief downtime for the workload. Taking a snapshot of the host's volumes first? Use "Create a volume snapshot" under EBS.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance to resize; the picker only lists instances already tracked in the estate's Terraform inventory, so the change cannot target an unmanaged host.

params.name: new_instance_type
params.label: New instance type
params.help: The EC2 instance_type, the size class that fixes the host's vCPU and memory; the list covers every size the estate already runs plus the engineer-approved resize steps around each, and applying the change stops and restarts the instance.

operations.title: Add a tag to an instance
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to this instance. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add one metadata tag key/value to an instance. Additive, reversible, no workload impact.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance to tag; the inventory picker limits the change to hosts Terraform already manages, and a tag is metadata that cannot affect the running workload.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Letters/digits/_/- , 2–65 chars. Governance keys are engineer-owned.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The text stored under the tag key, metadata only with no effect on the instance; limited here to 1-128 characters, safely inside AWS's 256-character tag-value limit.

operations.title: Provision a new EC2 instance
operations.summary: Request a brand-new server; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it is applied.
operations.description: Stand up a new host as a complete specification: size, image approach, network placement, access, storage and tags. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: host_name
params.label: Host name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like web-app-02. Names the server in the generated draft.

params.name: instance_type
params.label: Instance size
params.help: Size class for processor and memory — the same list the resize action offers, covering every family the estate runs.

params.name: ami
params.label: Machine image
params.help: The engineer picks and validates the image during review — approved images follow the account standard.

params.name: key_pair
params.label: Key pair
params.help: The key pair used for first access to the server.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet
params.help: Where the server lives on the network. The picker shows each subnet's zone and address range.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach this server. Pick one to five groups.

params.name: private_ip
params.label: Fixed private IP
params.help: Leave blank for an automatic address. Only set this when the workload needs a fixed one.

params.name: associate_public_ip
params.label: Public IP
params.help: Never assigned. These hosts are internal-only; this guardrail is written into the draft so the reviewer sees it.

params.name: iam_instance_profile
params.label: Permissions profile
params.help: The permissions profile the server runs with, from the profiles the estate already uses. Leave empty for the engineer to decide.

params.name: termination_protection
params.label: Termination protection
params.help: Always on for a new host — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: root_volume_gib
params.label: Root volume size (GiB)
params.help: System disk size. Data disks are added afterwards with the volume actions.

params.name: root_volume_type
params.label: Root volume type
params.help: New servers start on gp3 — faster baseline and cheaper per GiB than gp2.

params.name: root_encrypted
params.label: Root volume encryption
params.help: Always encrypted — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: imds_tokens
params.label: Instance metadata protection
params.help: Metadata access always requires session tokens on a new host — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Server name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What runs here
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this host.

decisions: Static IP or elastic IP strategy
Permissions profile when none is chosen

operations.title: Remove a tag from an instance
operations.summary: Remove a metadata tag from this instance. Reversible — you can add it back; governance tags are protected.
operations.description: Delete one metadata tag from an instance. Reversible — the tag can be re-added. Governance tags are protected.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance to remove the tag from; the inventory picker keeps the change scoped to estate-managed hosts, and a removed tag can always be re-added.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: The tag key to delete. Governance keys are engineer-owned.

operations.title: Toggle termination protection
operations.summary: Protect this instance from being terminated by accident, or lift that protection.
operations.description: Enable or disable disable_api_termination on an instance. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance whose termination-protection flag changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed hosts.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Protection
params.help: Sets disable_api_termination: when true, AWS rejects any terminate call from the console, API, or Terraform until the flag is turned off; only true/false are accepted, so nothing else on the instance can change.

operations.title: Toggle stop protection
operations.summary: Protect this instance from being stopped by accident, or lift that protection.
operations.description: Enable or disable disable_api_stop on an instance. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance whose stop-protection flag changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed hosts.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Protection
params.help: Sets disable_api_stop: when true, AWS rejects stop requests from the console or API, guarding an always-on workload; it is a strict true/false toggle that is reversible at any time.

operations.title: Toggle detailed monitoring
operations.summary: Turn on 1-minute CloudWatch monitoring for this instance, or drop back to the free 5-minute default.
operations.description: Enable or disable detailed CloudWatch monitoring (monitoring) on an instance. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime; a small cost when on.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance whose CloudWatch monitoring level changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed hosts.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Detailed monitoring
params.help: Sets monitoring: true publishes CloudWatch metrics at 1-minute detail for a small extra cost, false returns to free 5-minute basic metrics; either value leaves the workload itself untouched.

operations.title: Toggle source/destination check
operations.summary: Turn off the check that drops traffic not addressed to this instance, needed for NAT or routing hosts.
operations.description: Enable or disable source_dest_check. Disabling is required for NAT/routing instances — a networking change, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance whose source/destination check changes; the inventory picker limits this networking change to estate-managed hosts.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Source/dest check
params.help: Sets source_dest_check: true (the AWS default) makes the instance drop packets not addressed to itself, while false is needed only for NAT or routing appliances that forward other hosts' traffic, which is why this toggle is guardrailed.

operations.title: Set shutdown behavior
operations.summary: Choose what an in-guest shutdown does: keep the stopped instance, or permanently destroy it.
operations.description: Set instance_initiated_shutdown_behavior to stop or terminate. 'terminate' means an in-guest shutdown destroys the instance, so this is guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the EC2 instance whose OS-shutdown behavior changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed hosts.

params.name: behavior
params.label: On OS shutdown
params.help: Sets instance_initiated_shutdown_behavior, what AWS does when the operating system shuts itself down: 'stop' keeps the instance and its disks while 'terminate' destroys the instance, and the allowlist contains only these two AWS-defined values.

operations.title: Request another change to an EC2 instance
operations.summary: Request any other change to this instance not covered above; an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Anything not offered as a bounded form — security groups, IAM instance profile, user_data, tenancy, or a replacement. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: Select the instance your request is about so the engineer starts from the exact Terraform resource; the inventory picker keeps the request tied to an estate-managed host.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Describe it; an engineer will author the Terraform.

operations.title: Toggle public IP association
operations.summary: Turn public IP association on or off. Applying REPLACES the instance — new ID and a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set `associate_public_ip_address`. CAUTION: ForceNew on aws_instance — Terraform REPLACES the instance, not an in-place change. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Associate public IP
params.help: Toggle public IP association: true enables public ip association, false disables it.

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Toggle EBS optimization
operations.summary: Turn EBS optimization on or off. Applying REPLACES the instance — new ID and a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set `ebs_optimized`. CAUTION: `ebs_optimized` is ForceNew on aws_instance in provider 6.x — Terraform REPLACES the instance, it is NOT an in-place toggle. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: EBS optimized
params.help: Toggle EBS optimization: true enables ebs optimization, false disables it.

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Toggle primary IPv6 (GUA)
operations.summary: Turn on a primary IPv6 address for this instance. Turning it back off later can replace the instance, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set enable_primary_ipv6 (true/false) to assign a primary IPv6 GUA. Enabling is in-place; per the provider doc, DISABLING after it has been enabled forces instance recreation — engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Primary IPv6
params.help: Toggle primary IPv6 (GUA): true enables primary ipv6 (gua), false disables it.

operations.title: Toggle forced destroy of a protected instance
operations.summary: Allow this instance to be destroyed even with termination or stop protection on, a safety bypass, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set force_destroy (true/false, default false). When true it destroys the instance even if disable_api_termination or disable_api_stop is set — a guardrail bypass, so engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Force destroy
params.help: Toggle force_destroy: true enables force_destroy, false disables it.

operations.title: Toggle Windows password retrieval
operations.summary: Retrieve this instance's Windows administrator password into Terraform state — sensitive, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set get_password_data (true/false). When true, Windows administrator password data is retrieved and exported to Terraform state (password_data) — credential-equivalent material, so engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Get password data
params.help: Toggle get_password_data: true enables get_password_data, false disables it.

operations.title: Toggle hibernation support
operations.summary: Turn hibernation support on or off. Applying REPLACES the instance — new ID and a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set `hibernation`. CAUTION: `hibernation` is ForceNew on aws_instance — enabling or disabling it REPLACES the instance, it is NOT an in-place setting change. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Hibernation
params.help: Toggle hibernation support: true enables hibernation support, false disables it.

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Set instance tenancy
operations.summary: Change this instance's hardware tenancy: shared, dedicated, or a dedicated host. Applying REPLACES it, with a brief outage.
operations.description: Set `tenancy` (default/dedicated/host). CAUTION: ForceNew on aws_instance — Terraform REPLACES the instance. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: tenancy
params.label: Tenancy
params.help: Instance tenancy placement.

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Toggle instance replacement on user-data change
operations.summary: Decide whether a future startup-script edit replaces this instance instead of just restarting it; an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set user_data_replace_on_change (true/false, default false). When true, future user_data/user_data_base64 changes trigger a destroy-and-recreate instead of a stop/start — amplifies later blast radius, so engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Replace on user-data change
params.help: Toggle user_data_replace_on_change: true enables user_data_replace_on_change, false disables it.

operations.title: Set capacity reservation preference
operations.summary: Choose whether this instance can use any matching capacity reservation, or only on-demand capacity. Requires a brief stop to apply.
operations.description: Set capacity_reservation_specification.capacity_reservation_preference to open or none (default open). Per the provider doc, modifying it requires the instance to be in a stopped state — brief downtime; guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: preference
params.label: Reservation preference
params.help: Capacity-reservation targeting: open uses any matching reservation, none uses on-demand capacity.

operations.title: Set CPU AMD SEV-SNP
operations.summary: Turn confidential-compute memory encryption on or off. Applying REPLACES the instance, with a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set cpu_options.amd_sev_snp to enabled or disabled (M6a/R6a/C6a only). Per the provider doc, changing it destroys and recreates the instance; also a confidential-compute security feature — engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: amd_sev_snp
params.label: AMD SEV-SNP
params.help: AMD SEV-SNP memory encryption: enabled or disabled.

operations.title: Set CPU nested virtualization
operations.summary: Turn nested virtualization on or off. Applying stops and restarts the instance, and enabling turns off Virtual Secure Mode.
operations.description: Set cpu_options.nested_virtualization to enabled or disabled (8th-gen Intel only). Per the provider doc this is applied in-place but requires an instance stop/start; enabling also disables Virtual Secure Mode (VSM). Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: nested_virtualization
params.label: Nested virtualization
params.help: CPU nested virtualization: enabled or disabled.

operations.title: Set CPU credit mode
operations.summary: Choose how this burstable instance uses CPU credits: capped at standard, or unlimited for a small extra cost.
operations.description: Set credit_specification.cpu_credits to standard or unlimited (T2/T3 burst credits). Cost/performance implication, in-place, reversible, no downtime. Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: cpu_credits
params.label: CPU credits
params.help: Burstable-instance CPU credit mode: standard or unlimited.

operations.title: Toggle root volume delete-on-termination
operations.summary: Decide whether the root disk is deleted along with this instance when it's terminated, a data-loss risk, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set root_block_device.delete_on_termination (true/false, default true), controlling whether the root EBS volume is deleted when the instance terminates — a data-loss guardrail, so engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Delete root volume on termination
params.help: Toggle root volume delete-on-termination: true enables root volume delete-on-termination, false disables it.

operations.title: Set root volume encryption
operations.summary: Turn encryption on or off for the root disk. Applying REPLACES the instance — new ID and a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set root_block_device.encrypted (true/false, default false). Per the provider doc, modifying encryption on the root volume requires resource replacement (destroy/recreate) — engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Root volume encrypted
params.help: Set root volume encryption: true enables root volume encryption, false disables it.

operations.title: Add a tag to the root volume
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to this instance's root disk. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add one metadata tag key/value to the instance's root_block_device.tags map (applied post-creation via a separate API call). Additive, reversible, no downtime. Key/value bounds mirror the shipped ec2-add-instance-tag operation.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Letters/digits/_/- , 2–65 chars. Governance keys are engineer-owned.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The tag value (1–128 characters).

operations.title: Set root volume type
operations.summary: Change the root disk's storage type to adjust performance and cost. Applied live, no downtime.
operations.description: Set root_block_device.volume_type to one of standard, gp2, gp3, io1, io2, sc1, st1. Applied via online ModifyVolume — performance/cost implication, reversible, no downtime. Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: volume_type
params.label: Root volume type
params.help: EBS volume type for the root device (gp3/gp2 general-purpose SSD, io1/io2 provisioned IOPS, st1/sc1 HDD, standard magnetic).

operations.title: Set Nitro Enclaves
operations.summary: Turn Nitro Enclaves confidential computing on or off. Applying REPLACES the instance, with a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set enclave_options.enabled (true/false, default false). Per the provider doc, changing it destroys and recreates the instance; Nitro Enclaves is a confidential-compute security feature — engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Nitro Enclaves
params.help: Set Nitro Enclaves: true enables nitro enclaves, false disables it.

operations.title: Set instance auto-recovery
operations.summary: Turn automatic recovery from hardware failures on or off. Disabling removes that safety net.
operations.description: Set maintenance_options.auto_recovery to default or disabled. Disabling removes AWS automatic recovery on hardware impairment (removes a safety net). In-place, reversible, no downtime. Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: auto_recovery
params.label: Auto-recovery
params.help: Simplified automatic instance recovery: default (on where supported) or disabled.

operations.title: Set instance market type
operations.summary: Switch this instance's purchasing model, for example to Spot. Applying REPLACES it, with a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set `instance_market_options.market_type` (on-demand to spot or back). CAUTION: ForceNew on aws_instance — Terraform REPLACES the instance. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: market_type
params.label: Market type
params.help: Purchasing model for the instance.

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Set spot interruption behavior
operations.summary: Choose what happens when a Spot instance is interrupted: hibernate, stop, or terminate. Applying REPLACES the instance.
operations.description: Set `spot_options.instance_interruption_behavior`. CAUTION: this block is ForceNew on aws_instance — Terraform REPLACES the instance. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: instance_interruption_behavior
params.label: Interruption behavior
params.help: What happens when a Spot instance is interrupted: hibernate, stop, or terminate.

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Set spot request end date
operations.summary: Set the expiry date for a persistent Spot request. Applying REPLACES the instance, with a brief outage while it's recreated.
operations.description: Set `spot_options.valid_until` (persistent spot request end date, UTC). CAUTION: this block is ForceNew on aws_instance — changing it REPLACES the instance, not an in-place edit. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: valid_until
params.label: Valid until (UTC)
params.help: UTC timestamp, e.g. 2026-12-31T23:59:59Z

operations.downtime: EC2 instance replacement — downtime while the instance is destroyed and recreated; new instance ID and, without an Elastic IP, a new IP; instance-store data is lost

operations.title: Enable or disable the IMDS endpoint
operations.summary: Turn this instance's metadata service on or off. Disabling it can cut off credentials used by monitoring agents.
operations.description: Set metadata_options.http_endpoint to enabled or disabled (default enabled). Disabling IMDS can sever instance-profile credential retrieval for SSM/CloudWatch agents — engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: http_endpoint
params.label: IMDS endpoint
params.help: Whether the instance metadata service (IMDS) is reachable: enabled or disabled.

operations.title: Set IMDS IPv6 endpoint
operations.summary: Turn on or off IPv6 access to this instance's metadata service. Narrow scope, no downtime.
operations.description: Set metadata_options.http_protocol_ipv6 to enabled or disabled (default disabled), toggling the IPv6 IMDS endpoint. Narrow scope, still gated by http_tokens; reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: http_protocol_ipv6
params.label: IMDS IPv6 endpoint
params.help: Whether IMDS is reachable over IPv6: enabled or disabled.

operations.title: Set IMDS PUT response hop limit
operations.summary: Set how many network hops away this instance's metadata service can be reached. Raising it widens exposure.
operations.description: Set metadata_options.http_put_response_hop_limit to an integer from 1 to 64 (default 1). Secondary SSRF defense-in-depth knob — raising it widens reachability of the token endpoint. Reversible, no downtime. Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: hop_limit
params.label: Hop limit (1–64)
params.help: A value between 1 and 64.

operations.title: Set the IMDSv2 token requirement
operations.summary: Require session tokens for this instance's metadata service, or allow the weaker optional mode; an engineer reviews this security change.
operations.description: Set metadata_options.http_tokens to optional or required. 'required' enforces IMDSv2; 'optional' weakens the SSRF-based credential-theft boundary — engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: http_tokens
params.label: IMDS tokens
params.help: IMDS session tokens: required enforces IMDSv2, optional also allows IMDSv1.

operations.title: Set IMDS instance metadata tags
operations.summary: Turn on or off exposing this instance's tags through its metadata service. Minor information exposure, no downtime.
operations.description: Set metadata_options.instance_metadata_tags to enabled or disabled (default disabled), exposing instance tags via IMDS. Minor information-exposure consideration; narrow scope, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: instance_metadata_tags
params.label: Instance metadata tags
params.help: Whether instance tags are exposed through IMDS: enabled or disabled.

operations.title: Toggle DNS AAAA record responses
operations.summary: Turn on or off whether this instance answers DNS AAAA lookups for its name. Applying REPLACES the instance, with a brief outage.
operations.description: Set private_dns_name_options.enable_resource_name_dns_aaaa_record (true/false), controlling whether DNS AAAA queries for the instance hostname are answered — affects DNS-based discovery. This attribute forces replacement: the instance is destroyed and recreated (new ID, brief outage). Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Respond with AAAA records
params.help: Toggle DNS AAAA record responses: true enables dns aaaa record responses, false disables it.

operations.title: Toggle DNS A record responses
operations.summary: Turn on or off whether this instance answers DNS A-record lookups for its name. Applying REPLACES the instance, with a brief outage.
operations.description: Set private_dns_name_options.enable_resource_name_dns_a_record (true/false), controlling whether DNS A queries for the instance hostname are answered — affects DNS-based discovery. This attribute forces replacement: the instance is destroyed and recreated (new ID, brief outage). Guardrailed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Respond with A records
params.help: Toggle DNS A record responses: true enables dns a record responses, false disables it.

operations.title: Set private DNS hostname type
operations.summary: Choose how this instance's private DNS hostname is built: from its IP, or from its instance ID. Applying REPLACES it, with a brief outage.
operations.description: Set private_dns_name_options.hostname_type to ip-name or resource-name. On AMIs where cloud-init derives the OS hostname from IMDS, this can change the visible hostname — risky for hostname-bound licensing or host profiles, so engineer-reviewed.

params.name: instance
params.label: Instance
params.help: The instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: hostname_type
params.label: Hostname type
params.help: How the private DNS name is formed: ip-name (from the IP) or resource-name (from the instance ID).

operations.title: Add a tag to a key pair
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to this key pair. No effect on the key itself or any instance using it.
operations.description: Add one metadata tag key/value to an EC2 key pair (aws_key_pair `tags`). Additive and reversible, with no effect on the key material, SSH access, or any running instance that already uses the key.

params.name: target
params.label: Key pair
params.help: The key pair to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Letters/digits/_/- , 2-65 chars. Governance keys are engineer-owned.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The tag value (1–128 characters).
